name: Alphonse Mouzon
abstract: Alphonse Lee Mouzon (November 21, 1948 – December 25, 2016) was an American musician and vocalist, most prominently known as a jazz fusion drummer. He was also a composer, arranger, producer, and actor.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet
Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet
Billy Cobham and The Eleventh House, with Larry Coryell

works.t: The New Moon: Softly, as in a Morning Sunrise

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: you recorded with McCoy Tyner... paired up McCoy with Alphonse Mouzon... Afro-Blue, I remember that specifically, because that’s the opening cut, and it’s you.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah (laughs) he was quite a character, he was a good guy... he was willing to work with you... he worked with Chick and some other people-
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: So I called up Alphonze Mouzon and I said “what’s the deal here, man?” And he says “yeah, yeah, yeah you’re playing on it.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: I have this bass player Miroslav and Alphonse Mouzon and Joe Zawinul on piano... we’d love to have you on guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Erskine
about_quotes.evidence: my original Weather Report inspiration was hearing the way Alphonse Mouzon and Eric Gravatt played
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Erskine interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 9, 2020
